name: [BUG-SF-001] TC-SF-006 — GET /api/v1/fetch/status: No auth returns 200 (sensitive data exposed without auth)
description:
**[KNOWN BUG — BUG-SF-001]**

**What this test does:**
Calls GET /spr-fetcher/api/v1/fetch/status without any Authorization header and verifies the server rejects the request with 401.

**Expected:** HTTP 401 Unauthorized
**Actual:** HTTP 200 OK — returns the full status object with no auth required

**Sensitive data exposed without authentication:**
- accountNumber: the KCB bank account number used for all financial queries
- apiEndpoint: the full URL of the internal KCB upstream API (internal topology)
- tokenInjectionEnabled: reveals internal service configuration state
- mockEnabled: reveals whether the system is in mock/test mode

**Impact:** An external attacker with no credentials can enumerate the bank account number, the upstream KCB API URL, and internal service flags. This information can be used to craft targeted attacks against the KCB API or to understand the system architecture for further exploitation.
descriptionHtml:
[KNOWN BUG — BUG-SF-001]

What this test does:
Calls GET /spr-fetcher/api/v1/fetch/status without any Authorization header and verifies the server rejects the request with 401.

Expected: HTTP 401 Unauthorized
Actual: HTTP 200 OK — returns the full status object with no auth required

Sensitive data exposed without authentication:

  • accountNumber: the KCB bank account number used for all financial queries
  • apiEndpoint: the full URL of the internal KCB upstream API (internal topology)
  • tokenInjectionEnabled: reveals internal service configuration state
  • mockEnabled: reveals whether the system is in mock/test mode

Impact: An external attacker with no credentials can enumerate the bank account number, the upstream KCB API URL, and internal service flags. This information can be used to craft targeted attacks against the KCB API or to understand the system architecture for further exploitation.
